task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed OpenC3 COSMOS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A browser or application window showing the OpenC3 COSMOS dashboard or login screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent checked the initial telemetry for the targets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenC3 Telemetry Viewer displaying the 'HEALTH_STATUS' packet and 'CMD_ACPT_CNT' value for the 'INST' and 'INST2' targets.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent sent a command to the INST target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenC3 Command Sender interface showing a valid command (e.g., 'COLLECT' with 'TYPE' 'NORMAL') selected and sent to the 'INST' target.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent sent a command to the INST2 target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenC3 Command Sender interface showing a valid command selected and sent to the 'INST2' target.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent checked the final telemetry after sending commands.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenC3 Telemetry Viewer displaying the 'HEALTH_STATUS' packet with the updated/incremented 'CMD_ACPT_CNT' value for both 'INST' and 'INST2' targets.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent generated the structured JSON report with all required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the creation of a JSON file at '/home/ga/Desktop/fleet_command_report.json'. The content must include 'execution_timestamp' and a 'fleet_status' array with objects containing 'target' ('INST'/'INST2'), 'initial_cmd_acpt_cnt', 'final_cmd_acpt_cnt', 'delta', and 'verified' (boolean).

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used the OpenC3 COSMOS GUI to perform the audit rather than bypassing it.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the actual OpenC3 COSMOS web interface (Command Sender, Telemetry Viewer) being interacted with, not just terminal scripts or API calls.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent recorded real telemetry values instead of fabricating them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'initial_cmd_acpt_cnt' and 'final_cmd_acpt_cnt' values in the JSON report should logically correspond to the actual numbers visible in the Telemetry Viewer screenshots.

integrity.description: Verifies the JSON report is saved to the exact requested file path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor, terminal, or file manager explicitly shows the file path '/home/ga/Desktop/fleet_command_report.json' in the title bar, save dialog, or command prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file must be strictly located at '/home/ga/Desktop/fleet_command_report.json'. The target names to be audited are exactly 'INST' and 'INST2'. The task requires verifying the 'CMD_ACPT_CNT' from the 'HEALTH_STATUS' telemetry packet for both targets before and after sending commands.
